title: Project Hail Mary
kit_review: Embargo (statement)

title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest
kit_review: This is what people mean when they say they want yearning.

title: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl
kit_review: Name one thing that’s wrong with this movie. You can’t.

title: The Bluff
kit_review: Me like pirate.

title: Shelter
kit_review: I like how the “rules” of collateral damage in action movies are ignored, it makes an effort at keeping the stakes raised. Unfortunately the action is uninspired and I couldn’t care less about these people so it was all for naught.

title: The Devils
kit_review: Call me vain and proud, the greatest sinner to ever walk on God's Earth! But Satan's boy I could never be! I haven't the humility.It’s impossible to imagine this movie coming out today and yet it was released a mere three years after the abolition of the Hays Code. This movie confounds me in the best possible way, an intoxicating mixture of royal dramaturgy and the whimsy of your most obscure anime, a spinning wheel on which the obscene and the alluring couple like animals Ad Infinitum. Horny nu

title: Blades of the Guardians: Wind Rises in the Desert
Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie
kit_review: There is a joke in this that made me laugh harder than I’ve maybe ever laughed. Brilliant “how did they make this?” type stuff.

title: Crime 101
kit_review: Oh, did you notice it was like Heat? Here’s a cookie, you can eat it when you rewatch this badass movie.Halle Berry, holy shit. Cooked. Ate. Slayed. How many crumbs were left? You guessed it. Fuckin’ none. She put the ma in cinema. Chris Hemsworth with his socially awkward puppy dog routine combined with a Fincher-esque meticulous competence is the most interesting he’s been since Bad Times at the El Royale. Mark Ruffalo was born for these worn-out “I’m trying so hard but I don’t care anymore” c

title: Wuthering Heights
kit_review: Sad to report that neither my heights nor lows are wuthering. I remain unwuthered.

title: Good Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die
kit_review: The kind of unsubtle batshit bonkers but technically proficient Hail Mary that you’re either going to love it or hate it. But, like, you can’t hate Sam Rockwell (it’s illegal) so…

title: Marty Supreme
kit_review: I honestly can’t tell if Timmy’s been method acting on the press tour or if (more likely) everyone wants him to win an Oscar for playing a caricature of himself with a ping pong paddle. The last 15 minutes, though, were what some executives call “lean-in content.” I’m still chewing on a lot of things I can’t tell if I loved or hated, or should love because I hate them about the world/capitalism/myself. Forrest Gump would stomp Marty in a match, btw.

title: Cold Storage
kit_review: Super weird movie! It’s like another one of Liam Neeson’s straight-to-DVD movies but written by David Koepp and starring the endlessly charming (but less charming than usual) Joe Keery. Would have made a solid Tuesday night with a sack of weed and a trip to Red Box back in 2008.

title: Hamnet
kit_review: Sadness, like most feelings, is a spectrum. On one end there is a deep, impenetrable, vomit-inducing shock from which you may never recover. That’s the kind you don’t pass through unchanged. But there is also a cathartic depression, a passing gloom, even a grateful melancholy. It’s easy to make an audience sad. Hell, you can do that with a stranger on the street with a well-chosen photo. That’s not what is happening here, I reject that. There’s something spiritual, pagan, about this, an invocati

title: Crazy, Stupid, Love.
kit_review: THIS MOVIE IS FUCKING INSANE

title: Bring It On
kit_review: This was a capsule, man. Full review soon on the Streaming Things podcast.

title: The Wrecking Crew
kit_review: Decently fun on an airplane. Never thought I’d see Dave Bautista in a movie and think “should’ve been The Rock.” But here we are.

title: undertone
kit_review: Sound design was the MVP. It gives Pontypool vibes, with Insidious or Sinister type scares. The performances waver, the writing too, but people around me were really fucking scared and that made this a very fun viewing experience.

title: Cronos
kit_review: I got to see the restoration with Guillermo del Toro present to speak before and after the film. One of the highlights of my life. Also, the last film I will ever watch at Sundance in Park City. End of an era.

title: The Weight
kit_review: I knew nothing about this going in (the only way to do it) but if you had told me it was Sorcerer meets Cool Hand Luke but with an Ethan Hawke father-daughter twist I wouldn’t have been able to believe you. The audience was viscerally feeling every bone-crunching beat. A true throwback tale with a director-editor and a composer-writer, people putting their heart and soul into flawed characters you nonetheless wanna grow up to be. Loved it. Rockstar shit.

title: The Invite
kit_review: Olivia Wilde delivers direction so strong and a performance so good that she got a standing ovation at Sundance. That’s not a thing at Sundance! It’s not Cannes. It’s too cold here for that nonsense. I agonized for about 37 seconds about whether this was a 5 star or a 4 1/2 star picture and then I remembered nothing could matter less. I can say I loved this movie. The entire theater erupted in laughter more times than I could possibly count. It’s fucking hilarious. And then it slaps you in the f

title: The Gallerist
kit_review: Everyone seems to be acting in a different movie, with different levels of camp. Da’Vine Joy Randolph is great. Jenna Ortega is great. Maybe the worst Natalie Portman performance I’ve ever seen?

title: The Moment
kit_review: I now regret asking Charli about “Brat winter” after seeing the film. A very strong debut feature! Incredibly funny, was pleasantly surprised at the amount of British humor. Alexander Skarsgard is really having a blast in this. There are moments in life (and maybe this only true for a specific type of mental illness idk) where something SO wonderful is occurring and I’m not able to enjoy it at all because I’m already worrying about when it’s going to end and whether or not I deserved it at all.

title: Carousel
kit_review: Parts of this movie worked extremely well for me, but some did not. I love the subtext, the long spaces between bits of dialogue, but those were also occasionally excruciating. Jenny Slate is awesome.

title: The Rip
kit_review: It doesn’t rip. It’s fine. But it shoulda ripped. Some of the most entertaining and talented people working today all got together and made room-temp oatmeal. My heart is broken.

title: Night Is Short, Walk On Girl
Companion
kit_review: Bumped up a bit from my first watch, this is a really good script!
